---
title: "Data Wrangling with tidyverse"
author: 
  - name: Lindsay Hayes
    url: https://lindsaynhayes.github.io
    affiliation: Department of Cell Biology, OUHC
date: 2026-07-13
draft: false
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

# Pre-lecture activities

Before class, read the [pre-reading materials](/readings/index_R04.qmd):

# Lecture

## Learning objectives

::: callout-note
### Learning objectives

**At the end of class you will be able to:**
-   Get data in and out of R
-   Recognize and define an untidy dataset
-   Recognize and convert between long and wide data formats
-   Understand when to use both formats
-   Pipe functions together to perform multiple commands in sequence
:::

## Slides

-   [Lecture 04: Data Wrangling](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1Mz8Beb0KwJc6kDyX-DpwMsosjezn2D86bbsYu9N2lOU/edit?usp=sharing)

-   [In-Class Activity](/activities/classwork04/classwork_L04.qmd)

# Post-lecture Practice

Want to learn by listening? David Robinson a Data Scientist has a free lecture on [DataCamp](https://app.datacamp.com/learn/courses/exploratory-data-analysis-in-r-case-study). The first lecture on Data cleaning and summarizing with dplyr is free. You do need to sign up for a free datacamp account.
